title: Tocoyena longiflora
intro: Tocoyena longiflora, also known as the Long-flowered Tocoyena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tocoyena longiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tocoyena longiflora is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Tocoyena longiflora is classified under the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises a vast number of flowering plants, many of which are ornamental in nature. The genus Tocoyena is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Within the Rubiaceae family, Tocoyena longiflora is a member of the subfamily Ixoroideae. This classification highlights the plant's evolutionary relationship to other flowering plants within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Tocoyena longiflora is a species of flowering plant that has been traditionally used in various medicinal applications. Its roots and other parts have been employed in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. Tocoyena longiflora has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
